citations.source_title: Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic (CCBHC) Requirements for Community Needs Assessment
citations.source_agency_or_author: Third Horizon Strategies / SAMHSA criteria summary
citations.notes: Defines required CNA elements: service area, prevalence of mental health and SUD needs, economic factors and SDOH, cultures and languages, underserved populations, staffing-plan alignment, update plan, and stakeholder input.

citations.source_title: Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic (CCBHC) Community Needs Assessment, La Plata County, Colorado
citations.source_agency_or_author: Third Horizon for Axis Health System
citations.notes: Example of a completed CNA using ACS, BRFSS, CDC/NVSS/WONDER, CDC PLACES, HRSA, NCES, Census, SAMHSA Buprenorphine Practitioner Locator, and N-SUMHSS.

citations.source_agency_or_author: National Council for Mental Wellbeing / CCBHC-E NTTAC
citations.notes: Describes the CNA as the foundation of the CCBHC model and emphasizes mixed quantitative and qualitative data, stakeholder engagement, staffing, services, partnerships, and CQI.
Primary source for population, demographics, language, poverty, housing, insurance, transportation, and SDOH indicators.

citations.source_title: Local Area Unemployment Statistics via BLS Public Data API
citations.notes: Source for unemployment indicators where county series IDs can be constructed or supplied.

citations.source_title: CDC PLACES Local Data for Better Health
citations.source_agency_or_author: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
citations.notes: Source for modeled county/local health indicators such as frequent mental distress, depression, binge drinking, and smoking.

citations.source_title: CDC WONDER, National Vital Statistics System Mortality
citations.source_agency_or_author: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
citations.notes: Source for mortality indicators such as suicide and drug overdose; programmatic access may require manual query/download fallback.

citations.source_title: HRSA Data Warehouse and Area Health Resource Files
citations.notes: Source for HPSA, MUA/P, provider shortage, health center, and workforce/resource indicators.

citations.source_title: SAMHSA Behavioral Health Treatment Locator and N-SUMHSS
citations.source_agency_or_author: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
citations.notes: Source for treatment facility availability, MOUD-related provider/facility data, and behavioral health service infrastructure.

citations.source_type: Government data files / API where available
citations.notes: Source for school enrollment, district demographics, and school meal poverty proxy indicators.

citations.source_title: Point-in-Time Count and Housing Inventory Count
citations.source_agency_or_author: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
citations.notes: Source for homelessness indicators, often available by Continuum of Care rather than county.

citations.source_title: Data.Medicaid.gov and CMS Medicaid data resources
citations.notes: Potential source for Medicaid-related indicators, depending on geography and dataset availability.
Used for EHR/client demographics, service utilization, staffing, wait times, referrals, patient satisfaction, and qualitative findings.

errors: Could not detect latest ACS 5-year profile year; defaulting to 2023.
BLS LAUS returned no annual average observations for series LAUCN080670000000003.
